title: Double-free / use-after-free: `list.append(x)` under OOM double-frees the item (`_CALL_LIST_APPEND` steals `arg`, then `ERROR_NO_POP`)
description: list.append(x) under OOM double-frees the appended item. In the specialized _CALL_LIST_APPEND bytecode (Python/bytecodes.c), `arg` is stolen via PyStackRef_AsPyObjectSteal and handed to _PyList_AppendTakeRef, which consumes the reference on every path -- on the grow path it decrefs the item when list_resize fails under OOM (_PyList_AppendTakeRefListResize, listobject.c:531). The uop then takes ERROR_NO_POP(), leaving the already-consumed `arg` stackref on the value stack; the eval loop's exception_unwind (generated_cases.c.h:13853-13857) then PyStackRef_XCLOSEs it, decreffing the item a SECOND time -> double-free. When the item has another live owner (a __slots__ attribute in the minimal repro; an os.DirEntry path field in the fleet vehicle), that owner is left dangling -> use-after-free, detected as _Py_NegativeRefcount on debug/JIT builds and SIGSEGV on the upstream release build. Generic and pure-Python reproducible; os.DirEntry/os.scandir was only the discovery vehicle.
notes: ROOT-CAUSED to _CALL_LIST_APPEND (Python/bytecodes.c): the uop does `_PyList_AppendTakeRef(self, PyStackRef_AsPyObjectSteal(arg)); if (err) ERROR_NO_POP();`. The steal consumes arg's ref; _PyList_AppendTakeRef consumes it on every path (decrefs newitem when list_resize fails under OOM -- listobject.c:531); ERROR_NO_POP then leaves the dead arg stackref on the value stack, and exception_unwind (generated_cases.c.h:13853-13857) PyStackRef_XCLOSEs it -> second decref -> double-free of the item. Fix: don't leave the stolen arg on the stack on the error path (pop/account for the consumed input, or steal only on success). AUDIT: scanned all 440 ops in bytecodes.c for steal+ERROR_NO_POP -- _CALL_LIST_APPEND is the ONLY affected op (the EX-call ops _DO_CALL_FUNCTION_EX/_PY_FRAME_EX call INPUTS_DEAD();SYNC_SP() before ERROR_NO_POP; DELETE_DEREF's TakeRef is on a cell, not a stack input; the comprehension adds LIST_APPEND/SET_ADD/MAP_ADD steal but use the codegen-accounted ERROR_IF -- empirically [e.a for e in items] via LIST_APPEND, the SAME _PyList_AppendTakeRef helper, does NOT crash where out.append(e.a) via CALL_LIST_APPEND does). Confirmed 4 ways: ASan free-stack (item freed by PyStackRef_XCLOSE<-exception_unwind; GIL+ASan build ~/projects/3.16_debug_asan_pymalloc run with PYTHONMALLOC=malloc -- NB the dir name is a misnomer: ASan forces WITH_PYMALLOC=0, the env var is accepted because it is a GIL build, not because of pymalloc; free-threaded ASan builds reject PYTHONMALLOC=malloc), listobject.c source (decref-on-resize-failure), disassembly (out.append(e.a) -> CALL_LIST_APPEND), and a control (load e.a but don't append -> NO crash). GENERIC + pure-Python: a __slots__ class is enough (E.a holds the 2nd ref; see repro.py), no os.scandir/filesystem/C-type. DirEntry was only the discovery vehicle (the original fleet crash was stack.append(e.path) in os.walk; entry->path was the 2nd ref, crash detected at DirEntry_dealloc). Determinism: ft_debug_asan GIL on/off; SEGV on upstream release (real memory-safety bug). NATURAL TRIGGER confirmed (no _testcapi -- list_resize fails only on a genuine alloc failure): a real RLIMIT_AS address-space cap -> SEGV at out.append(x), 3/3 on the upstream release build (faulthandler pins the crash to the append line); control under the same cap with the failure NOT on a list-append grow raises a clean catchable MemoryError, no segv. So a program that catches MemoryError can still be left double-freed/UAF'd under real memory pressure. See repro_natural.py. Warm-up f() load-bearing: specializes CALL_LIST_APPEND + builds code objects so the shallow OOM-0003 doesn't mask it. Found by OOM Phase-4 --oom-seq fleet (vehicle zoneinfo._tzpath) -- unreachable by the single-call fail-forever harness (trips OOM-0003 first). Related to OOM-0005 (same eval-loop stackref-over-close-under-OOM class -- a steal-then-non-popping-error anti-pattern -- but a DIFFERENT op/site; kept separate, the pattern is worth auditing across specialized call/append uops). Dedup: the fleet vehicle keys on DirEntry_dealloc@posixmodule.c:16199, but this is generic so other victims surface at other dealloc sites and won't auto-dedupe. STRONG self-contained upstream candidate (tiny pure-Python repro, release-crashing, one-spot fix). FILED as python/cpython#151818 (its own issue). RELATED but DISTINCT: #151119 + open PR #151538 target the same _PyList_AppendTakeRef-under-OOM area but a different defect -- the missing eval-stack SP-sync (the _Py_Dealloc stackpointer!=NULL assert in LIST_APPEND when the appended item has no 2nd ref -> immediate dealloc). PR #151538 marks the append ops HAS_ESCAPES so it ALSO adds the SP-sync to _CALL_LIST_APPEND, BUT its _CALL_LIST_APPEND hunk only adds the sync and still JUMP_TO_ERROR()s with the stolen arg on the stack (diff: 6 SetStackPointer lines added, 0 ERROR_NO_POP/ERROR_IF accounting changes), so THIS double-free survives #151538 -- consistent with the audit (CALL_LIST_APPEND is the lone append op using ERROR_NO_POP; LIST_APPEND/SET_ADD/MAP_ADD use the codegen-accounted ERROR_IF). Cross-referenced on #151818.
